title: Apple Pencil Buyer's Guide: Which Model Should You Choose?
content:
Apple now offers four different Apple Pencil models at $79, $99, and $129 price points, each with different feature sets and compatibility. Our guide helps you decide which ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå is best for you.


Which ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå you should buy is firstly determined by which iPad model you have. Some ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå models are only compatible with one kind of ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå, while others are compatible with two. If your ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå model only supports one kind of ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå, your buying decision is straightforward. Use the table below to find out which ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå models your ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå supports:






































‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå (first-generation, 2015) ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå (second-generation, 2018) ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå (USB-C, 2023) ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå Pro (2024)
iPad Pro 12.9-inch (first- and second-generation) ‚ÄåiPad Pro‚Äå 12.9-inch (third-, fourth-, fifth-, and sixth-generation) ‚ÄåiPad Pro‚Äå 12.9-inch (third-, fourth-, fifth-, sixth-, and seventh- generation) ‚ÄåiPad Pro‚Äå 13-inch (seventh-generation)
‚ÄåiPad Pro‚Äå 9.7-inch and 10.5-inch ‚ÄåiPad Pro‚Äå 11-inch (first-, second-, third-, and fourth-generation) ‚ÄåiPad Pro‚Äå 11-inch (first-, second-, third-, fourth-, and fifth-generation) ‚ÄåiPad Pro‚Äå 11-inch (fifth-generation)
iPad Air (third-generation) ‚ÄåiPad Air‚Äå (fourth- and fifth-generation) ‚ÄåiPad Air‚Äå (fourth-, fifth-, and sixth-generation) ‚ÄåiPad Air‚Äå (sixth-generation)
iPad mini (fifth-generation) ‚ÄåiPad mini‚Äå (sixth-generation) ‚ÄåiPad mini‚Äå (sixth-generation)
‚ÄåiPad‚Äå (sixth-, seventh-, eighth-, ninth-, and tenth-generation) ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå (tenth-generation)



Simply put, if your ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå features a Lightning connector, it is only compatible with the first-generation ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå, providing it has ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå support at all. It will not work with the second-generation ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå or the ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå with USB-C. If your ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå features a USB-C port, it is compatible with the second-generation ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå and the ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå with USB-C, except the tenth-generation ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå, which supports the first-generation ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå and ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå with USB-C, and the M2 ‚ÄåiPad Air‚Äå and M4 ‚ÄåiPad Pro‚Äå, which support the ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå Pro and ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå with USB-C.

It is worth noting that the first-generation ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå is only compatible with the tenth-generation ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå using a USB-C cable and USB-C to ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå Adapter. It is not possible to use the first-generation ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå this way with any other ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå model that has a USB-C port.

All four ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå models feature "pixel-perfect" precision, low latency, and tilt sensitivity, but feature different designs, pairing and charging methods, pressure sensitivity capabilities, and more. The below table lists all hardware and feature differences between the different ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå models.







































































































‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå (first-generation, 2015) ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå (second-generation, 2018) ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå (USB-C, 2023) ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå Pro (2024)
Glossy finish Matte finish Matte finish Matte finish
Weighted to prevent rolling Flat edge to prevent rolling and snap to the side of an ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå Flat edge to prevent rolling and snap to the side of an ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå Flat edge to prevent rolling and snap to the side of an ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå

Lightning connector USB-C port

Barrel roll

Haptic feedback
Find My support

$99 $129 $79 $129



First-Generation Apple Pencil vs. Apple Pencil With USB-C


Most users should choose the ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå with USB-C. For a price of just $79, the ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå with USB-C provides a good balance of features with a more modern design. It also has the added advantage of being compatible with a wide range of models and will likely work with all new ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå models for many years to come.

It is only worth opting for the first-generation ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå if you specifically want pressure-sensitivity for activities like illustration, since the ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå with USB-C does not have this feature. Buyers should be aware that this model comes at a $20 premium, requires a USB-C to ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå Adapter with the tenth-generation ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå, and will not be compatible with any future ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå models. It also cannot be magnetically attached to an ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå for storage, features a larger and older design, and has a removable cap over the Lightning connector that's easy to lose.

Second-Generation Apple Pencil or Apple Pencil Pro vs. Apple Pencil With USB-C


If choosing between the second-generation ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå or ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå Pro and the ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå with USB-C, the second-generation and Pro models are a better choice on account of their superior feature set, but this requires spending an additional $50. Double-tap to change tools and pressure-sensitivity may be particularly useful additions for illustrators, while the easy pairing and charging on the side of an ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå provides a much better experience than a wired connection with a USB-C cable.

If you have only basic needs for activities like handwriting notes and marking up documents, the ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå with USB-C is still a great option at a much more affordable price point. It even supports ‚ÄåApple Pencil‚Äå hover on the newer ‚ÄåiPad Air‚Äå and ‚ÄåiPad Pro‚Äå models.
